zh.lifeSummary: 當Roger Hill於1547年出生在英國薩默塞特朋迪斯福德時，他的父親Sir William Hill22歲，母親Lady Agnes Trowbridge Hill17歲。他於1568年5月1日與Mary Hassard在英國薩默塞特比特明斯特結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他去世於1608年12月22日在英國薩默塞特陶頓享年61歲，埋葬在英國薩默塞特陶頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Roger Hill于1547年出生在英国薩默塞特朋迪斯福德时，他的父亲Sir William Hill22岁，母亲Lady Agnes Trowbridge Hill17岁。他于1568年5月1日与Mary Hassard在英国薩默塞特比特明斯特结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他去世于1608年12月22日在英国薩默塞特陶頓享年61岁，埋葬在英国薩默塞特陶頓。

nameMeanings.name: Hill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill, from Middle English hill, hell, hull ‘hill’ (Old English hyll). Compare Hiller . This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English: possibly in some cases from the personal name Hille, a pet form of some name such as Hilger or Hillary .
German: from a short form of Hildebrand or any of a variety of other names, male and female, containing ancient Germanic hild ‘battle’ as the first element.

variants.name: Hillock
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name for someone who lived ‘(by the) small hill’, from Middle English hillok, a diminutive of hille (Old English hyll ‘hill’). See Hill .

variants.name: Hills
meanings: English: variant of Hill with genitival -s if a relationship name, attested in Matilda Hilles, 1327 (Somerset), where the genitival form may signify widowhood; or with plural -s if habitational, attested in Johanne de Hyles, 1379 (Bickerstaffe, Lancashire), and Ricardo del Hyles, 1379 (Great Eccleston, Lancashire); or, with post-medieval excrescent -s, probably the most common form.

variants.name: Hiller
meanings: German: metronymic from Hille, a pet form of the female personal name Hildegund (see Hilke ).
German: from a masculine personal beginning with the ancient Germanic element hild ‘strife, battle’.

variants.name: Hil
meanings: Dutch (Van den Hil): topographic name from hil, hul ‘hill’, particularly in the sense of a flight hill above sea level in the southwestern Netherlands.

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Roger Hill was born in 1547, in Pondisford, Somerset, England, his father, Sir William Hill, was 22 and his mother, Lady Agnes Trowbridge Hill, was 17. He married Mary Hassard on 1 May 1568, in Pitminster, Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 7 daughters. He died on 22 December 1608, in Taunton, Somerset, England, at the age of 61, and was buried in Taunton, Somerset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Roger Hill est né en 1547, à Pondisford, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, Sir William Hill, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Lady Agnes Trowbridge Hill, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Mary Hassard le 1 mai 1568, à Pitminster, Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 7 filles. Il est décédé le 22 décembre 1608, à Taunton, Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterré à Taunton, Somerset, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Roger Hillが1547年にイギリスソメルセトポンディスフォードで生まれた時，父Sir William Hillは22歳，母Lady Agnes Trowbridge Hillは17歳であった。1568年5月1日にイギリスソメルセトピトミンスターでMary Hassardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1608年12月22日にイギリスソメルセトタウントンで61歳で死去し，イギリスソメルセトタウントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Roger Hill(이)가 1547년 영국 서머세트 폰디스퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Hill(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Agnes Trowbridge Hill(은)는 17세였다. 그는 1568년 5월 1일 영국 서머세트 피트민스터에서 Mary Hassard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1608년 12월 22일 영국 서머세트 타운턴에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 서머세트 타운턴에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Mary Hassard em 1 de maio de 1568, em Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 7 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Roger Hill родился в 1547 г. в местности пондисфорд, сомирсет, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir William Hill было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Agnes Trowbridge Hill, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Hassard 1 мая 1568 г. в местности питминстир, сомирсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 7 дочерей. Он умер 22 декабря 1608 г. в местности таунтон, сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 61 год и был похоронен в местности таунтон, сомирсет, Англия.
